validationModes.strict: Blocking validation mode for no-new-debt gates.
validationModes.audit: Diagnostic validation mode for migration discovery and future-state rules.

externalDependencies.namespace_prefixes: Exact namespace or child namespace prefix match against referenced external types.
externalDependencies.type_prefixes: Full referenced type-name prefix match for targeted vendor/framework APIs.
limitations: matches only referenced type metadata visible from project types
does not perform full method-body analysis
does not statically analyze third-party package internals

layerMatching.namespace: Exact namespace or child namespace prefix match.
layerMatching.namespace_suffix: Optional suffix constraint applied after namespace prefix matching.

contractFamilies.validates: A source layer does not reference forbidden target layers or configured legacy runtime namespace prefixes.
Layers are ordered outermost to innermost; dependencies may point only toward later layers.
A source layer references only itself and explicitly allowed first-party layers.
Directed dependency cycles among selected layers.
Forbidden method-body calls using Roslyn source scanning and IL fallback scanning.
Unity .asmdef references under Assets, including editor-only references and forbidden assembly prefixes.
Mutual independence among a set of layers; no selected layer references another selected layer.
Protected layers are referenced only by explicitly allowed importer layers. Self-references within the protected layer are implicitly allowed.
A source layer does not reference forbidden vendor/framework dependency groups declared in external_dependencies.

Reusable layer order contract applied to multiple namespace containers. Each container produces one concrete ordered-layer contract. Relative layer names are resolved by prepending the container namespace. Optional layers (layers[].optional: true) silently skip empty namespaces. When exhaustive is true, every immediate child namespace under the container that contains types must be mapped to a declared layer; unmapped sibling namespaces produce a violation.

A type selected by constrained matchers (name suffix/prefix, namespace, layer, base type, implemented interface, attribute) resides in a declared layer/namespace/project/assembly and/or carries a declared naming suffix/prefix. must_reside_in_projects resolves to assembly-name matching via project discovery, not physical .csproj-membership tracking. Requires at least one placement or naming expectation.

An assembly's exported (public/protected/protected-internal) types and directly-declared members match a normalized signature allowlist (declared_api). assemblies must be non-empty. Undeclared exported types/members are violations. forbid_public_constants_unless_declared adds an independent, stricter check: an exported const field is forbidden unless its fully-qualified name is in allowed_public_constants, even if its full signature is already in declared_api. Detects only undeclared additions, not removed/changed signatures; not a substitute for binary/package compatibility validation.

A declared attribute type (matched by exact fully-qualified name via attributes, and/or by prefix via attribute_prefixes) is restricted to (allowed_only_in_*) or excluded from (forbidden_in_*) a declared layer/namespace/project/assembly. Scans the type itself and every declared member (ctor/method/property/field/event) regardless of visibility. Requires at least one attribute selector and at least one location expectation. Static marker placement validation only: does not evaluate attribute constructor arguments/named properties (not authorization/security correctness validation) and does not detect the absence of a required marker (required-marker enforcement is a deferred follow-up).

A type residing in a declared source surface (source_layers and/or source_namespaces prefixes) does not inherit, directly or transitively through its base-class chain, from a forbidden base type (matched by exact fully-qualified name via forbidden_base_types, and/or by prefix via forbidden_base_type_prefixes). Constructed generic base types are matched by their generic type definition's CLR name (e.g. App.Repository`1). Requires at least one source surface selector and at least one base type selector. Class inheritance only; interface implementation restrictions belong to interface-implementation contracts.

A non-interface type implementing a declared interface (matched by exact fully-qualified name via interfaces, and/or by prefix via interface_prefixes, including interfaces inherited through base classes; constructed generic interfaces matched by their generic type definition's CLR name) resides only in (allowed_only_in_*) or outside (forbidden_in_*) declared layers/namespaces/projects/assemblies. Requires at least one interface selector and at least one location expectation. Static type-relationship validation only: does not resolve runtime dependency-injection registrations.

A type residing outside a declared composition boundary (allowed_only_in_layers/namespaces/projects/assemblies) does not call a forbidden composition-root/service-locator API (matched with the same call-pattern vocabulary as method-body contracts: exact member names, Type.Member names, fully qualified members, and namespace/type prefixes via forbidden_apis). Requires at least one forbidden_apis selector and at least one allowed_only_in_* composition boundary selector. Reflection/IL-based static detection of call sites only: does NOT validate runtime dependency-injection resolution and does not prove every service is registered correctly.

exceptionMechanisms.allowed_types: Exact full type-name exceptions for dependency and allow-only contracts.
exceptionMechanisms.ignored_violations: Narrow source_type and forbidden_reference baselines with exact or glob-like matching; each entry should include a reason.

unsupportedCapabilities: custom contract families outside the documented YAML schema
authoringWarnings: Start from real assemblies and namespaces, not idealized architecture names.
Use strict rules only when they pass today or represent an accepted no-new-debt gate.
Use audit rules for migration discovery and future-state architecture.
Do not add broad ignored violations such as source_type: '*' unless explicitly accepted as temporary migration debt.
The current YAML loader ignores unmatched properties; use the JSON Schema to catch unsupported fields.
